question: Are group 2 innate lymphoid cells ( ILC2s ) increased in chronic rhinosinusitis with nasal polyps or eosinophilia?
paragraph: Yes, group 2 innate lymphoid cells (ILC2s) are increased in chronic rhinosinusitis with nasal polyps and eosinophilia. The evidence indicates that ILC2s are implicated in driving Th2 inflammation in chronic rhinosinusitis and are correlated with the presence of nasal polyps, high tissue eosinophilia, and increased blood eosinophilia. Their frequency is associated with clinical disease characteristics, including nasal symptom severity and nasal polyps. In patients with eosinophilic chronic rhinosinusitis, ILC2s are notably increased and are correlated with the disease, especially in those with co-existing asthma. These cells contribute to eosinophil recruitment and activation, further supporting their increased presence in eosinophilic disease subsets.
